Description: Test with standard, well-formatted company names
Test with null, empty, or whitespace-only inputs
Test with special characters and symbols
Test with misspelled or incomplete company names
Test with generic names matching multiple entities
Test different case variations of company names
All mandatory and optional fields populated
Only some fields populated for lesser-known entities
No data generated for non-existent companies
Critical fields present, optional fields missing
Example Test Scenarios: • Verify: Company Name, Category always present
• Allow: Awards, ESG Practices to be empty
• Test classification of mandatory vs optional

Description: Related fields populated together or empty together
Example Test Scenarios: • If CEO Name exists, CEO LinkedIn should exist
• If funding exists, investors should exist

Description: Verify against known ground truth data

Example Test Scenarios: • Year of Incorporation ≤ CEO tenure start
• Total funding = sum of all rounds

Description: Ability to trace data back to sources
Detection of invented people, events, or data

Example Test Scenarios: • CEO name sounds real but is wrong person
• Office locations exist but company not there

Description: Wrong data presented with high certainty

Example Test Scenarios: • Specific numbers that can't be confirmed

Description: Derived fields match their source values

Example Test Scenarios: • Burn Multiplier = Net Burn ÷ Net New ARR

Description: Fields logically align with each other

Example Test Scenarios: • Currency: all USD or mixed with notation
• High hiring velocity + low turnover = growth
• Large employee size + many offices = scale
• High NPS + low churn = customer satisfaction

Description: Very large numbers at upper bounds
Legitimate zero values in numeric fields
Ratios with special meaning at extremes
Correct data types for each field
Example Test Scenarios: • Numbers stored as numbers, not strings

Description: Events after LLM training data cutoff

Example Test Scenarios: • Recent acquisitions not in training data
• Test: IBM vs International Business Machines
• Test: AT&T vs American Telephone & Telegraph
• Test: 3M vs Minnesota Mining and Manufacturing
• Test: Meta Platforms, Inc. vs Meta

Description: Generation time for different company types

Example Test Scenarios: • Compare: Public vs private company time
• Test: Company A then Company B
• Verify: No data from A in B's profile

Description: Graceful handling when data doesn't exist
Fields that don't apply to entity type
Unclear if data exists or just not found
Example Test Scenarios: • Data might exist but not public
• If Revenue = null, YoY Growth = null

Description: Primary vs secondary vs tertiary sources
Example Test Scenarios: • Tier 1: Official company website, SEC filings
